title: Differences Between P_MSWINX and P_X11 Output
breadcrumbs: Differences Between P_MSWINX and P_X11 Output
text: Differences Between P_MSWINX and P_X11 Output For enhanced printing, Uniface provides the P_MSWINX device translation table for Windows and the P_X11 table for PostScript. However, there are differences in print output produced, especially in the way colors and images are handled. Table 1. Functionality P_MSWINX P_X11 Color printing Note: Color printing is only possible if Print using colors is selected in the Print Job Model. Only index colors can be printed. Index colors can be printed. Web colors (which are set using the Background and Foreground widget properties) can be printed. Note: It is not possible to combine index colors and web colors in a single component. Color code 0 in labels Transparent background Background is Uniface color 0 Labels Labels can be printed in bold, italic or with underline. Labels cannot be printed in bold, italic or with underline. Text is printed in the normal font. Underscores indicating shortcuts In a label, a character that is preceded by a % sign, is rendered onscreen as an underscore. Label is printed without an underscore Label is printed with a literal % sign. Text wrapping in buttons Text that does not fit the button is wrapped. Text that does not fit the button is clipped. Image aspect ratio The image is clipped to the field borders, ignoring the Preserve Aspect property. The image fits the field width, and the image aspect ratio is maintained. Images scaling in percentage Not supported Not supported Transparent images Background is black Background is transparent Related Links Device Translation Tables for Printing Printer device tables are a collection of sequences (control codes) associated with specific logical functions (such as bold, form feed, and init). The sequences begin with the escape character and are referred to as escape sequences. Color Handling The objects that make up an application's user interface have color properties that determine foreground and background colors. These properties can be set in the Uniface IDE when defining each object, but they can also be set dynamically using ProcScript. Depending on the property, you can specify an object's color as a system color name, web color name, or RGB value (expressed as decimal or hexadecimal values). Image Handling Uniface can display and print pictures that are stored as image files on disk, as glyphs, or BLOBs in a database. The type of picture can affect performance. Was this topic helpful?
